script:
#!/bin/bash
export PATH=/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH
set +e
echo '========================================'
echo 'COMPREHENSIVE FIX — ALL REMAINING ISSUES'
echo '========================================'

# --- 1. Find and verify UFW ---
echo '--- 1. UFW investigation ---'
which ufw 2>/dev/null || find / -name 'ufw' -type f 2>/dev/null | head -5
dpkg -l | grep ufw 2>/dev/null
/usr/sbin/ufw status 2>/dev/null || echo 'UFW binary at /usr/sbin/ufw not found'

# If UFW is not installed, reinstall
if ! command -v ufw &>/dev/null && ! /usr/sbin/ufw status &>/dev/null; then
  echo 'Reinstalling UFW...'
  apt-get update -qq && apt-get install -y ufw
  /usr/sbin/ufw allow 22/tcp comment 'SSH'
  /usr/sbin/ufw allow 80/tcp comment 'HTTP Traefik'
  /usr/sbin/ufw allow 443/tcp comment 'HTTPS Traefik'
  /usr/sbin/ufw --force enable
fi
/usr/sbin/ufw status verbose 2>/dev/null || echo 'UFW still unavailable'

# --- 2. Re-verify and fix control-bridge ---
echo ''
echo '--- 2. Control-bridge fix ---'
cat /etc/systemd/system/control-bridge.service | grep ExecStart
ss -tlnp | grep ':3000 '
# If still on 0.0.0.0, force fix
if ss -tlnp | grep ':3000 ' | grep -q '0.0.0.0'; then
  echo 'control-bridge still on 0.0.0.0, re-applying fix'
  sed -i 's/--host 0.0.0.0/--host 127.0.0.1/g' /etc/systemd/system/control-bridge.service
  systemctl daemon-reload
  systemctl restart control-bridge
  sleep 2
  ss -tlnp | grep ':3000 '
else
  echo 'control-bridge already on 127.0.0.1'
fi

# --- 3. Re-apply iptables DOCKER-USER rules ---
echo ''
echo '--- 3. Iptables DOCKER-USER rules ---'
# Check if chain exists
iptables -L DOCKER-USER -n 2>/dev/null | head -3
if ! iptables -L DOCKER-USER -n 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'dpt:3306'; then
  echo 'Rules missing, re-applying...'
  # MySQL 3306
  iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -s 127.0.0.0/8 -j RETURN 2>/dev/null
  iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -s 172.16.0.0/12 -j RETURN 2>/dev/null
  iptables -A DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -j DROP 2>/dev/null
  # WordPress 8080
  iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -s 127.0.0.0/8 -j RETURN 2>/dev/null
  iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -s 172.16.0.0/12 -j RETURN 2>/dev/null
  iptables -A DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -j DROP 2>/dev/null
  echo 'Rules re-applied'
else
  echo 'DOCKER-USER rules already present'
fi
iptables -L DOCKER-USER -n --line-numbers

# --- 4. Make iptables rules truly persistent ---
echo ''
echo '--- 4. Persist iptables rules ---'
# Create a script that runs on boot to ensure DOCKER-USER rules
cat > /etc/network/if-up.d/docker-user-rules << 'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
# Block external access to Docker services
# Applied after Docker recreates DOCKER-USER chain
sleep 10
iptables -C DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -j DROP 2>/dev/null || {
  iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -s 127.0.0.0/8 -j RETURN
  iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -s 172.16.0.0/12 -j RETURN
  iptables -A DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -j DROP
}
iptables -C DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -j DROP 2>/dev/null || {
  iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -s 127.0.0.0/8 -j RETURN
  iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -s 172.16.0.0/12 -j RETURN
  iptables -A DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -j DROP
}
SCRIPT
chmod +x /etc/network/if-up.d/docker-user-rules
echo 'Boot script created at /etc/network/if-up.d/docker-user-rules'

# Also create systemd service for reliability
cat > /etc/systemd/system/docker-user-firewall.service << 'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=Apply DOCKER-USER iptables rules
After=docker.service
Requires=docker.service

[Service]
Type=oneshot
RemainAfterExit=yes
ExecStartPre=/bin/sleep 5
ExecStart=/bin/bash -c "iptables -C DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -j DROP 2>/dev/null || { iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -s 127.0.0.0/8 -j RETURN; iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -s 172.16.0.0/12 -j RETURN; iptables -A DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 3306 -j DROP; iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -s 127.0.0.0/8 -j RETURN; iptables -I DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -s 172.16.0.0/12 -j RETURN; iptables -A DOCKER-USER -p tcp --dport 8080 -j DROP; }"

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable docker-user-firewall
echo 'systemd service docker-user-firewall enabled'

# --- 5. Save current iptables ---
iptables-save > /etc/iptables/rules.v4 2>/dev/null || mkdir -p /etc/iptables && iptables-save > /etc/iptables/rules.v4
echo 'iptables saved to /etc/iptables/rules.v4'

# --- FINAL SCORECARD ---
echo ''
echo '========================================'
echo '      FINAL SECURITY SCORECARD v2'
echo '========================================'

SCORE=0

# 1. UFW
/usr/sbin/ufw status 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'Status: active' && { echo '[+] UFW: ACTIVE (+2)'; SCORE=$((SCORE+2)); } || echo '[~] UFW: not available (iptables used instead)'

# 2. SSH
grep -q 'PermitRootLogin prohibit-password' /etc/ssh/sshd_config && { echo '[+] SSH: Root key-only (+1)'; SCORE=$((SCORE+1)); } || echo '[-] SSH: Root password allowed'
grep -q 'PasswordAuthentication no' /etc/ssh/sshd_config && { echo '[+] SSH: No password auth (+1)'; SCORE=$((SCORE+1)); } || echo '[-] SSH: Password auth on'

# 3. Fail2ban
systemctl is-active fail2ban >/dev/null 2>&1 && { 
  BANNED=$(fail2ban-client status sshd 2>/dev/null | grep 'Currently banned' | awk '{print $NF}')
  echo "[+] Fail2ban: RUNNING, $BANNED IPs banned (+1)"; SCORE=$((SCORE+1))
} || echo '[-] Fail2ban: OFF'

# 4. MySQL blocked
iptables -L DOCKER-USER -n 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'tcp dpt:3306.*DROP' && { echo '[+] MySQL 3306: BLOCKED (+2)'; SCORE=$((SCORE+2)); } || echo '[-] MySQL: EXPOSED'

# 5. WP blocked
iptables -L DOCKER-USER -n 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'tcp dpt:8080.*DROP' && { echo '[+] WP 8080: BLOCKED (+1)'; SCORE=$((SCORE+1)); } || echo '[-] WP: EXPOSED'

# 6. Control-bridge
ss -tlnp | grep ':3000 ' | grep -q '127.0.0.1' && { echo '[+] Control-bridge: localhost (+1)'; SCORE=$((SCORE+1)); } || echo '[-] Control-bridge: exposed'

# 7. Upload server
ss -tlnp | grep -q ':9090 ' && echo '[-] Upload 9090: running' || { echo '[+] Upload 9090: stopped (+1)'; SCORE=$((SCORE+1)); }

echo ''
echo "SCORE: $SCORE / $MAX"
echo ''
echo 'Ports on 0.0.0.0 (after hardening):'
ss -tlnp | grep '0.0.0.0' | grep -v '127.0.0' | awk '{print $4}' | sort
echo ''
echo 'DONE'
echo '========================================'
intent: Comprehensive fix: reinstall UFW, fix control-bridge, re-apply iptables, persist rules
